title: Spicy Flaky Peruvian Prawns Stew
description: A comforting, low‑calorie Peruvian stew that turns raw tiger prawns into silky flakes of flavor, spiced with ají amarillo and cumin, all pressure‑cooked to lock in freshness.
instructions: Rinse the raw tiger prawns and pat them dry; set aside.
Heat the olive oil in the pressure‑cook pot over medium heat and sauté the onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for another 30 seconds.
Stir in the ají amarillo paste, cumin and smoked paprika, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the fish broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the prawn shells (optional) and cover with the coconut milk; stir well.
Seal the pressure‑cook pot and cook on high pressure for 10 minutes.
Quick‑release the pressure, open the pot, and stir in the fresh prawns; cook on sauté mode for 2‑3 minutes until they turn pink and flaky.
Finish with lime juice, season with salt to taste, and serve hot.
Optionally garnish with chopped cilantro or sliced avocado for extra flavor.
